message: I've been thinking about blackouts lately—not in a panic way, just the practical kind of thinking. Turns out there's actually a lot of simple stuff we can do ahead of time that makes the whole thing way less stressful when the power does go out. I found a really solid resource from the Red Cross that breaks down the basics in a no-nonsense way, and I'm sharing it because I think it's exactly the kind of thing that's worth skimming when you have five minutes. What's the one thing you'd most want to have ready if the lights went out for a day or two?
